description: A clear downward emissions trend across the reporting period, all scopes.

expected_response.executive_summary: Total approved emissions for Q1 2026 were 512.30 tCO2e across 340 records, split between Scope 1 (298.10 tCO2e) and Scope 2 (214.20 tCO2e).
key_highlights: Emissions declined for five consecutive months
Grid electricity is the largest single activity source at 214.20 tCO2e
expected_response.trend_explanations: Monthly emissions fell steadily from 610.40 tCO2e in November 2025 to 498.10 tCO2e in March 2026, a sustained downward trend across the reporting period.
recommendations: Investigate which specific sites or activities drove the November-to-March decline, to assess whether it can be sustained or replicated.

description: A scope-filtered report showing a rising trend -- the narrative must explain a worsening figure honestly, not just a positive one.

expected_response.executive_summary: Scope 1 emissions for Q1 2026 totaled 298.10 tCO2e across 210 records, entirely from diesel stationary combustion.
key_highlights: Scope 1 emissions rose every month from November 2025 through March 2026
Diesel stationary combustion is the sole Scope 1 activity source in this period
expected_response.trend_explanations: Scope 1 emissions increased steadily from 240.00 tCO2e in November 2025 to 298.10 tCO2e in March 2026, a sustained upward trend driven entirely by diesel stationary combustion.
recommendations: Review diesel stationary combustion activity for the sites driving this increase to identify potential efficiency or fuel-switching opportunities.

description: A thin, single-month, low-record-count period -- confidence must reflect the limited data rather than overstating certainty.

expected_response.executive_summary: Approved emissions for March 2026 totaled 12.40 tCO2e across only 6 records, all from diesel stationary combustion.
key_highlights: Only 6 approved records fall within this single-month period
All emissions in this period are Scope 1 diesel stationary combustion
expected_response.trend_explanations: Only one month of data is available in this period, so no month-over-month trend can be assessed.
recommendations: Widen the reporting period to at least a full quarter before drawing trend conclusions from this data.
